conditions.name: Septic Arthritis

questions.questionText: Do you have fever WITH a hot, swollen knee AND inability to bear weight?
questions.question: Do you have fever WITH a hot, swollen knee AND inability to bear weight?

effects.redFlagText: MEDICAL EMERGENCY: features suggest septic arthritis. Seek immediate emergency care — joint aspiration is required.
MEDICAL EMERGENCY: features suggest septic arthritis. Seek immediate emergency care — joint aspiration is required.

questions.questionText: Is there a history of recent infection (skin, urinary, respiratory)?
questions.question: Is there a history of recent infection (skin, urinary, respiratory)?

questions.questionText: Any recent joint surgery, injection, or prosthesis?
questions.question: Any recent joint surgery, injection, or prosthesis?

questions.questionText: Any diabetes, HIV, or immunosuppression?
questions.question: Any diabetes, HIV, or immunosuppression?

conditions.clinical_note: MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Diagnosis is clinical + laboratory; joint aspiration is the gold standard. AVOID aggressive manual testing.

questions.question: Is the pain worse when descending stairs than ascending?

options.value: No - it is localized to the inferior pole of the kneecap

effects.notes: Localized inferior-pole pain suggests patellar tendinopathy.

questions.question: Did you feel the kneecap move out of place?

questions.question: Have you had repeated episodes of the kneecap dislocating?

conditions.clinical_note: Imaging: lateral + axial X-rays; MRI if MPFL tear suspected.

conditions.name: Knee Fracture

questions.questionText: Are you unable to bear weight on the leg since the injury?
questions.question: Are you unable to bear weight on the leg since the injury?

effects.redFlagText: Inability to bear weight after trauma — strong fracture indicator; urgent X-ray.
Inability to bear weight after trauma — strong fracture indicator; urgent X-ray.

questions.questionText: Are you unable to perform a straight leg raise (lift the leg straight)?
questions.question: Are you unable to perform a straight leg raise (lift the leg straight)?

effects.redFlagText: Inability to straight-leg-raise — strong indicator of patella fracture / extensor disruption.
Inability to straight-leg-raise — strong indicator of patella fracture / extensor disruption.

questions.questionText: Was there a direct blow to the front of the knee?
questions.question: Was there a direct blow to the front of the knee?

conditions.clinical_note: Imaging REQUIRED: X-ray AP/Lateral/Skyline; CT for tibial plateau detail; MRI for soft tissue.

conditions.name: Baker's Cyst

questions.questionText: Do you notice swelling or a lump behind the knee?
questions.question: Do you notice swelling or a lump behind the knee?

questions.questionText: Do you feel tightness behind the knee?
questions.question: Do you feel tightness behind the knee?

questions.question: Do you have calf swelling or calf pain without injury?

effects.redFlagText: Calf swelling/pain — possible ruptured Baker's cyst vs DVT; assess urgently.
Calf swelling/pain — possible ruptured Baker's cyst vs DVT; assess urgently.

questions.questionText: Do you have a history of knee OA or meniscus injury?
questions.question: Do you have a history of knee OA or meniscus injury?

effects.notes: Baker's cyst is usually secondary to intra-articular pathology.
Baker's cyst is usually secondary to intra-articular pathology.

conditions.clinical_note: Usually secondary to OA or meniscus pathology — treat the underlying cause.
